reasoning:
The prompt requires estimating the probability that ABBV's closing price will be higher than 217.4107 (as of November 9, 2025 closure, we are using the last traded price). The last close before the cutoff was $217.41.
  
The dates are:
2025-11-16 (1 week out)
2025-12-09 (1 month out)
2026-02-07 (3 months out)

1) Stock momentum and baseline: After a mixed Q3 earnings report on Oct 31, where ABBV beat EPS & revenue but GAAP EPS fell due to an IPR&D charge, the stock plunged from ~$226 down to $210 by Nov 3. However, it quickly rallied the rest of the week to close at $217.41. The underlying news is quite good: 2025 guidance was raised, Q3 beat, dividend was increased to $1.73/share.
2) Based on the short-term recovery, the 1-week outlook (Nov 16) has a solid chance of continuing upward, though an even higher probability is not warranted given typical volatility and random walk nature of stocks over 1 week. Yfinance linear model projected 55%. I assign 53%.
3) Over 1 month (Dec 09), the positive guidance update and raised expectations should allow it to continue to drift upward, as market reassesses the "sell the news" reaction. I give it 56%.
4) Over 3 months (Feb 07), there is an ex-dividend date around Jan 15, which will knock the price down mechanically by $1.73 (~0.8%). While the general stock drift should push it higher over 3 months, this specific dividend drop, combined with increased variance over a longer horizon which usually pulls probabilities closer to 50%, makes me slightly reduce it from the 1-month forecast to 55%.
